Notes: Verbatim: "Limited Partners in the fund include the Dutch entrepreneurial development bank FMO, Proparco, ImpactAssets, Ford Foundation, MetLife, and Mastercard." Final close $61.6M on 2025-09-08. Slug accion-venture-lab-fund-ii added to peer_funds.yml as a sibling to the evergreen accion-venture-lab umbrella; scraper fund_lp_scraper_AccionVentureLabII.py created (uses PR Newswire mirror because accion.org is Cloudflare-blocked).

Rockefeller Foundation surfaces as LP via its Zero Gap Fund vehicle. Verbatim quote on Rockefeller Foundation page: "LeapFrog's Emerging Consumer Fund III (Fund III) is a growth equity fund serving low-income consumers in Asia and Africa, incorporating a blended finance insurance product to mitigate tail-end risks." Zero Gap Fund's $3M commitment is structured as an insurance deductible backstopping a broader policy benefiting Fund III equity investors. Fund flipped from pending_onboard to active; scraper fund_lp_scraper_LeapFrogIII.py created.

Hewlett operates as a grant-only philanthropy ($631M grants in 2024); financials page discloses Form 990-PF + real-estate partnerships only, no PRI/MRI program comparable to Ford or MacArthur. Searched: hewlett.org/grants, hewlett.org/news, "Hewlett Foundation" "limited partner" impact fund, "Hewlett Foundation" PRI "program-related investment". Cross-checked all 80+ tracked active/pending impact funds — no Hewlett LP role found. PitchBook hints at endowment-side LP positions but these are mainstream PE/VC behind paywall, not INGO-impact-fund commitments.

WKKF Mission Driven Investments program is fully disclosed (~24 fund commitments, vintages 2010-2023) but exclusively US-domestic, racial-equity / Working Families / Thriving Children focus. Zero overlap with INGO/global-South impact-fund universe. Closest cousins for operator reference: Illumen Capital (US bias-aware FoF), Social Finance (US Pay-for-Success outcomes-fund manager — closest analog to BAT SDG / Bridges SDG), Impact America Fund (US working-class tech VC). No international microfinance / off-grid energy / smallholder ag / water / refugee / outcomes commitments disclosed. Cross-referenced against all ~80 tracked funds — no match.

Surdna ~$240M MRI/PRI portfolio is exclusively US racial-equity / CDFI-adjacent: Impact America Fund I ($1M, 2016) + Fund III, Illumen Capital ($1M 2018 + $1M 2021), Fair Food Fund ($5M debt, 2014), Founders First, Blackstar Stability Fund, Vamos Ventures, Impact Engine. Themes housing + financial inclusion pursued via US-only vehicles outside the INGO/global impact-fund universe. Secondary source: surdna.org/impact-report-2018-2024/, ImpactAlpha Don Chen Q&A. No grant or LP edge to any tracked fund found in 4 fetches.

37 publicly disclosed impact/aligned investments — climate/clean-energy/housing mainstream impact PE/VC + ESG public equity. Examples: Generation Sustainable Solutions Funds II-IV, Vision Ridge Sustainable Asset Funds II/III, Stonepeak Global Renewables Fund I, TPG Alternative & Renewable Technologies Fund I (distinct from TPG Rise II), Encourage Capital, North Sky Capital, HitecVision, True Green Capital, RRG Sustainable Water Impact Fund, G2VP, DBL Partners IV, Ninety One Global Environment Fund. "Parametric Calvert ESG Defensive Equity" references Calvert Research/Eaton Vance ESG indexing — NOT Calvert Impact Capital Community Note. Zero overlap with tracked INGO impact-fund universe. Confirms skill-doc presumption of out-of-scope.

MACP (Margaret A. Cargill Foundation + Anne Ray Foundation, ~$8.5B AUM) does not publicly name LP commitments to any specific impact fund. Reported MRI bucket of ~$100M as of Dec 2015 (GIIN profile thegiin.org/member/margaret-a-cargill-philanthropies/) but zero named fund vehicles. PRIs described only as "$5.3M to three certified Native CDFIs." Mission Investors Exchange profile gated (HTTP 403). Searched: macphilanthropies.org/news, /about/our-grantmaking, /annual-report-2022, /resources/financials. Cross-checked all 80+ tracked active/blocked/pending funds against MACP + (Acumen, WaterEquity, Mercy Corps, Conservation International, AgDevCo, Mirova) — no co-occurrence in primary press releases. Unblock path: ProPublica Nonprofit Explorer Form 990-PF (EIN 37-1758406) Schedule of Investments line-items.

OSF surfaces via its impact-investment arm SEDF. Verbatim attribution: "The Soros Economic Development Fund (SEDF), the impact investment arm of the Open Society Foundations, is investing $7.5 million in EcoEnterprises Fund IV." Press release dated 2025-12-08. EcoEnterprises Fund IV is the $150m successor vintage to ecoenterprises-iii (already tracked); added to peer_funds.yml as ecoenterprises-iv and flipped to Pipeline Status=active. Coalition LPs verbatim from same source: FinDev Canada, FMO, IDB Invest, International Finance Corporation, Proparco, JICA, SIFEM, Visa Foundation, Wire Group. Scraper fund_lp_scraper_EcoEnterprisesIV.py created (10 rows). Secondary opportunity (not pursued this round): SEDF $7m into Persistent Africa Climate Venture Builder Fund — distinct vehicle from tracked persistent-energy-capital.

Omidyar Network and its split-out vehicles (Luminate, Imaginable Futures, Humanity United, Flourish Ventures, Omidyar Network India) operate a hybrid LLC/501(c)(3) direct-equity model — they invest directly into companies, not as LPs into funds. Searched: omidyar.com/how-we-invest/our-funder-partnerships, omidyar.com/news, FY19 audited financial statements (PDF) — confirmed they hold LP positions in private equity / VC funds (~$2.3M unfunded commitments as of 2019) but the audited statements do not name specific underlying funds publicly. Cross-checked acumen.org/institution-partners (Omidyar absent), thecatalystfund.com/our-story (Flourish appears only on Investor Advisory Committee, not as LP), bfaglobal.com/catalyst-fund/press-kit-catalyst-fund. Indian-fund searches (Aavishkaar, Omnivore, Quona) showed direct co-investment patterns, not LP relationships. Existing foundation_lps.yml entry has empty lp_commitments block — confirms posture.

CZI (LLC + 501(c)(3) hybrid) does not publicly LP into any impact fund. Endowment page describes a "manager of managers approach" seeking "outstanding risk-adjusted returns" — conventional institutional money management with NO fund names disclosed. CZI Ventures (chanzuckerberg.com/grants-ventures/ventures/) is direct-equity into ~67 companies (Eruditus, Andela, ed-tech/biotech/AI), not a fund-of-funds. The TPG Rise mention in the Decade of Impact report (chanzuckerberg.com/newsroom/ventures-impact-investing-report-2025/) is a CO-INVESTOR relationship in Eruditus Series F ("with participation from existing investors Softbank Vision Fund 2, Leeds Illuminate, Accel, CPP Investments and the Chan Zuckerberg Initiative"), NOT CZI as LP into Rise. Cross-checked Acumen/LeapFrog/BlueOrchard/Mercy Corps Ventures/Mirova/Aavishkaar — zero primary-source LP attribution. ProPublica 990-PF for CZI Foundation (EIN 45-5002209) summary view does not surface Schedule of Investments line-items. Confirms skill-doc presumption of out-of-scope.

Bloomberg Philanthropies operates as a grant-maker (~$4.3B/yr). Closest fund-adjacent vehicle is the Climate Innovation & Development Fund (CIDF), but the primary-source language explicitly characterises it as GRANT capital, not LP: "Goldman Sachs and Bloomberg Philanthropies partnered to seed $25 million in grant capital and launch the Climate Innovation & Development Fund." CIDF is ADB-managed concessional finance, not a tracked INGO/peer fund. PipelineRoad LP-Allocator profile lists Bloomberg as private-markets allocator but discloses NO specific fund names, managers, or commitments. Searches across "Bloomberg Philanthropies" + LP + (LeapFrog | Acumen | Mirova | Persistent Energy | Nithio) — zero hits. Bloomberg Family Foundation 990-PF (EIN 20-5602483) holds private equity positions but no impact-fund line-items surfaced. Bloomberg NEF and Climate Finance Leadership Initiative are research/convening platforms, not investment vehicles. /environment/driving-sustainable-finance/ returned 403; manual operator review recommended if a future LP-style commitment appears. Confirms skill-doc presumption of grant-only/out-of-scope.

Yield Giving (MacKenzie Scott) operates strictly as an unrestricted-grant maker. Strongest near-miss is the LIIF $30M Dec-2024 award, but verbatim primary-source language is "the largest one-time unrestricted grant the organization has ever received" — grant to LIIF the org, not LP capital into a LIIF fund vehicle. Yield Giving self-describes its model as "trust- and entity-based giving at scale" (yieldgiving.com/essays/investing/). Per Inside Philanthropy and Devex (devex.com/organizations/lost-horse-llc-199841), any direct impact investments flow through Lost Horse LLC, Scott's Seattle/Delaware family-office LLC, which by design avoids the disclosure regime that would surface fund commitments. Searched yieldgiving.com gifts table (JS-rendered, not extractable via WebFetch — residual uncertainty), Acumen/Global Partnerships/BRAC/Heifer/Mercy Corps, "Lost Horse" + "limited partner"/"anchor investor" — zero LP attributions. Existing foundation_lps.yml entry has empty lp_commitments — confirms posture.

DATA-QUALITY FLAG for operator review: no operating "CIFF US" entity exists today. The historical US affiliate (EIN 04-3632641, West Hartford CT) filed its final return in 2022 and successor-entity rebranded as Big Win Philanthropy. CIFF (UK), already on /network/ as childrens-investment-fund-foundation, announced Oct-2025 it will pull funding from US-based groups entirely (devex.com/news/major-foundation-pauses-grants-to-us, philanthropynewsdigest.org). Cross-Border Impact Ventures Jan-2024 final-close release names "Children's Investment Fund Foundation" but absent US/UK qualifier — almost certainly attributable to CIFF UK ($6.6B impact-investing entity), not the wound-down US 501(c)(3). Searched: ciff.org/about-us, ProPublica EIN 04-3632641, instrumentl 990 reports, Cross-Border Ventures press, Devex/Alliance/PND coverage. Recommend operator: (a) merge ciff-us slug into existing childrens-investment-fund-foundation node, OR (b) remap to Big Win Philanthropy if successor in scope. No tracked-fund LP edge findable.

Wellcome Trust runs a £37.6B endowment with ~28-32% in PE/VC (~£8.3B per PEI), but public disclosure is limited to direct public equity holdings — no fund-level LP positions named publicly. PE book is mainstream venture/buyout/life-sciences VC tilt per PEI and Top1000Funds; pitchbook.com/profiles/limited-partner/10366-57 lists 264 commitments behind paywall. Charitable side runs as GRANTS, not LP: Climate Impacts Awards (£2.5M each, not-for-profit recipients only), GEN-IMPACT (£10M), DELTAS Africa (£64.5M to Science for Africa Foundation), Wellcome Leap programs. Searched Acumen / LeapFrog / BlueOrchard / Catalyst Fund Resilience / BIX / SIMA / ResponsAbility — zero LP attribution. wellcome.org/about-us/investments returned 403 to direct fetch. Confirmed grant-side activity uses charitable instruments; LP-side activity is mainstream institutional VC outside the INGO/global-South impact-fund universe. Examples in their PE book: mainstream venture/buyout funds; specific names not publicly disclosed.

NNF (~$170B AUM) and its 100%-owned asset manager Novo Holdings A/S deploy capital as (a) direct grants from the Foundation and (b) direct equity by Novo Holdings into life-sciences/biotech/planetary-health companies. No public LP commitments to external INGO/global-South impact funds in tracked universe. "Impact" vehicles are CAPTIVE/self-managed, not LP positions: REPAIR Impact Fund (USD 165M AMR-focused, "established by Novo Holdings A/S in February 2018", repair-impact-fund.com/about), Planetary Health Investments (~USD 5B planned direct-investment strategy launched 2024, novoholdings.dk/investments/planetary-health-investments), Novo Holdings Asia/Venture/Growth/Seed/Principal (all direct equity). Grant-side examples checked: USD 100M global-health partnership w/ Wellcome+Gates 2024, USD 57M to WHO, DKK 200M pandemic preparedness at DTU, up to USD 25M to CARB-X 2024 — all grants. Cross-checked LeapFrog / BlueOrchard / Acumen / Mirova / SIMA LP rosters — no NNF/Novo Holdings appearance. Captive-fund + direct-equity + grant model puts NNF outside the INGO/global-South LP-fund universe.

Laudes Foundation surfaces as Anchor Investor of the Good Fashion Fund (FOUNT-managed, Fashion for Good sub-advised). Verbatim coalition sentence in the Fashion for Good press release: "Rabobank is the first senior debt investor to the fund and joins Laudes Foundation and the Mills Fabrica as co-investors in the first investment fund focused only on driving the implementation of innovative solutions in the fashion industry." Independent corroboration on goodfashionfund.com (Partners section tags Laudes Foundation under Anchor Investor with verbatim CEO Leslie Johnston quote: "We are therefore proud to be an anchor investor in the Good Fashion Fund..."), but FFG-press-release URL chosen as scrape target because goodfashionfund.com renders Mills Fabrica and Rabobank tags via client-side JS. Fund slug good-fashion-fund added to peer_funds.yml and flipped to Pipeline Status=active; scraper fund_lp_scraper_GoodFashionFund.py created (3 rows: Laudes Foundation, The Mills Fabrica, Rabobank). Initial close September 2019, ~USD 19m blended structure, India + Bangladesh textile manufacturing supply-chain focus.

Jacobs Foundation surfaces as joint anchor (with Swiss Agency for Development and Cooperation, SDC) of the Impact-Linked Fund for Education (ILFE), an open-ended impact-linked finance vehicle deploying SIINC and Impact-Linked Loans to EdTech enterprises in West Africa and MENA. Verbatim attribution on Jacobs Foundation activity page: "The Jacobs Foundation and the Swiss Agency for Development and Cooperation (SDC) have jointly committed CHF 6 million in the new Impact-Linked Fund for Education." Independent corroboration on ilf-fund.org/programs-and-facilities/impact-linked-fund-education/: "In December 2021, this open-ended fund secured commitments of CHF 6 million from Swiss-based Jacobs Foundation and the Swiss Agency for Development and Cooperation (SDC) in a first closing." Co-managed by iGravity and Roots of Impact under the Impact-Linked Finance Fund (ILFF) Stichting umbrella. Entity-disambiguation confirmed: Jacobs Foundation (Zürich charitable, founded 1989) is the named party — distinct from Jacobs Holding AG (commercial) and Jacobs Douwe Egberts (which appears as IDH Farmfit corporate LP under a different entity). EOF (Education Outcomes Fund) priority hypothesis was checked but unverified — ECONNREFUSED on edoutcomesfund.org and no primary source naming Jacobs as EOF outcome funder. Fund slug impact-linked-fund-education added to peer_funds.yml and flipped to Pipeline Status=active; scraper fund_lp_scraper_ILFE.py created (2 rows: Jacobs Foundation, SDC).

Luminate (UK-based philanthropy split out of Omidyar Network in 2018) operates a hybrid 501(c)(3) + LLC grant-making model and explicitly does not LP into funds. Verbatim from luminategroup.com/funding-faq: funding mechanisms are "Unrestricted and restricted grants, including fiscally sponsored grants" and "Project funding through our LLC"; further: "We no longer provide equity investments or loans, except for potential follow-on investments to current partners." Luminate has actually narrowed scope further than Omidyar — discontinued new equity/loans entirely. Thematic out-of-scope confirmed: four pillars are Civic Empowerment, Data & Digital Rights, Financial Transparency, Independent Media (per luminategroup.com/about) — categorically disjoint from INGO impact-fund universe (microfinance, smallholder ag, climate/energy, water, refugee, gender). Cross-checked all 80+ tracked active/blocked/pending fund slugs — zero plausible thematic or LP overlap. Investees are media outlets, civic-tech NGOs, digital-rights advocacy groups, not commingled investment funds. Confirms skill-doc presumption of out-of-scope and inherits Omidyar Network's prior NO_LP outcome. Recommend not re-attempting.

Citi Foundation 501(c)(3) (EIN 13-3781879) operates strictly as a grantmaker (~$55M/yr). Four active programs — Community Finance Innovation Fund (a Foundation-run grant initiative, NOT an external LP vehicle despite the name), Community Progress Makers, Global Innovation Challenge, Pathways to Progress — all flow as grants to nonprofits. Searched: citigroup.com/global/foundation, citigroup.com/global/foundation/programs, ProPublica/Instrumentl 990-PF (no PRI line items surfaced), "Citi Foundation" + LP/limited partner/impact fund, "Citi Foundation" + (Mercy Corps Ventures | WaterEquity | Acumen | LeapFrog | Women's World Banking | Accion). Accion has been a Citi Foundation GRANTEE since 1982 — recurring grants, not LP capital. Near-misses excluded per entity disambiguation: Citi Impact Fund ($500M direct equity, 2020 — Citigroup balance-sheet capital, not Foundation), Citi Inclusive Finance / Citi Social Finance (commercial-bank lending — $20M loan to ASA Philippines, DFC-Citi loan to CRDB Bank Tanzania — bank credit, not Foundation), Action for Racial Equity $50M Black Entrepreneurs allocation (sits inside Citi Impact Fund / bank, not Foundation). Confirms grant-only posture; matches skill-doc presumption that corporate-bank foundations operate as grantmakers while their parent banks operate as LPs/lenders.

JPMorgan Chase Foundation 501(c)(3) (EIN 23-7049738, ~$1.2B assets, ~$202M FY2024 grants) operates as a grantmaker. Searched: jpmorganchase.com/impact, ProPublica 990-PF index (no LP/PRI line items in indexed summaries), CB Insights investor profile (9 tracked items, single disclosed = $0.5M grant to OATS Oct 2025, no fund LP commitments), BFA/Catalyst Fund partner pages, OFN Finance Justice Fund coverage, JPMC + (MicroBuild | Accion | WaterEquity | Mercy Corps Ventures | WWB) cross-checks. Closest near-miss is Catalyst Fund Resilience (in tracked list): JPMC committed £12M/$15M circa 2020, but verbatim attribution is "J.P. Morgan" / "JPMorgan Chase & Co." — the BANK, not the Foundation — so excluded per disambiguation. Finance Justice Fund: $20M debt from JPMorgan Chase Community Development Banking (the bank) + $2.35M grant from the Foundation — Foundation only made a grant, not LP/PRI commitment. PRO Neighborhoods, AdvancingCities, Entrepreneurs of Color Fund: even if Foundation participated, these are US-domestic CDFI vehicles → OUT_OF_SCOPE regardless. JPMC's impact-investing capital flows through JPMorgan Chase & Co. Impact Finance team (since 2018), JPMorgan Asset Management DFI, J.P. Morgan Social Finance, and Community Development Banking — all explicitly excluded by entity-disambiguation rules. Confirms grant-only posture for the Foundation 501(c)(3).

Bank of America Charitable Foundation operates as a grantmaker (Neighborhood Builders, Workforce Development, Community Volunteer Awards, economic-mobility programs). Form 990-PF expenditures overwhelmingly grants to recipients including Acumen Fund, Calvert Impact Capital, Opportunity Finance Network, LISC — program grants, not PRI/LP. Searched: about.bankofamerica.com/en/making-an-impact, /en/making-an-impact/economic-mobility, ProPublica 990-PF for the Foundation EIN, Calvert Impact Capital Community Note investor lists, tracked fund press releases (WaterEquity, WWB, LeapFrog, EcoEnterprises, &Green, MCV, Goodwell, Bamboo). Where "Bank of America" appears in fund press releases or Calvert investor lists, primary-source attribution is to Bank of America N.A. (CRA-eligible Treasury allocations) or Bank of America Community Development Corporation (BACDC) — bank/subsidiary, NOT the Foundation. Headline BofA impact-capital activity ($1.25B Equality Progress Sustainability Bond, $2B Equity Progress, CDFI/MDI equity, LIHTC investments) all flows through Bank of America Corporation or BACDC — explicitly excluded by entity disambiguation. Acumen receives BofA Foundation grant support but is not disclosed as receiving an LP commitment into ALEG/KawiSafi/ARAF/H2R Amplify from the Foundation. Even where the bank/BACDC LPs into US CDFI/LIHTC vehicles, those are US-domestic and OUT_OF_SCOPE for the INGO/global-South universe regardless of attribution. Confirms grant-only posture for the Foundation; LP capital under the BofA umbrella sits with the bank or BACDC.

Nuveen (TIAA asset management arm) surfaces as one of 50 LPs of LeapFrog Emerging Consumer Fund III via the Impact Investing Institute case study. Verbatim: "The fund is backed by 50 limited partners (LPs) including insurers (including Prudential Financial and AIG), other institutional investors (including Morgan Stanley and Nuveen), DFI/MDBs (including CDC, IFC and EIB) and many foundations/endowments (including Ford Foundation and Rockefeller Foundation)." ECF III final close May 2019 (43M). Entity disambiguation: this is Nuveen the institutional capital provider committing into an externally-managed LeapFrog vehicle (a true LP edge), NOT a Nuveen-managed captive impact strategy. Source choice rationale: nuveen.com does not publicize this LP commitment; LeapFrog/BusinessWire 2019 announcements timed out today; ImpactInvest (UK govt-backed Institute) page is the strongest stable primary disclosure. Existing fund_lp_scraper_LeapFrogIII.py extended to multi-source (Rockefeller URL retained for Rockefeller Foundation, Impact Investing Institute URL added for Nuveen + Prudential Financial); 3 rows now emitted (Rockefeller Foundation, Nuveen, Prudential Financial).

Prudential Financial Impact Investments surfaces as one of 50 LPs of LeapFrog Emerging Consumer Fund III via the Impact Investing Institute case study. Verbatim: "The fund is backed by 50 limited partners (LPs) including insurers (including Prudential Financial and AIG), other institutional investors (including Morgan Stanley and Nuveen), DFI/MDBs (including CDC, IFC and EIB) and many foundations/endowments (including Ford Foundation and Rockefeller Foundation)." Entity disambiguation: "Prudential Financial" verbatim refers to Prudential Financial, Inc. (NYSE: PRU) the US insurer / asset manager whose Impact Investments program (~B+ committed since 1976) is the LP-acting capital — distinct from the Prudential Foundation 501(c)(3) (grant-only) and from Prudential plc / M&G plc (UK). The 2022 investor.prudential.com Alexander Forbes joint announcement with LeapFrog independently corroborates a strategic Prudential Financial / LeapFrog institutional relationship. Secondary opportunity (not pursued this round): leapfroginvest.com/press-release/leapfrog-raises-over-1bn-in-fourth-fundraising-cycle/ names Prudential Financial as a "cornerstone investor" in LeapFrog Fund IV (.15bn, 2023) — distinct vehicle not in current tracked-list. Same Impact Investing Institute source used for the Nuveen edge in the same batch; LeapFrog III scraper now multi-source.

MetLife Foundation surfaces as named equity co-owner of Habitat for Humanity MicroBuild Fund. Verbatim from Habitat's own program page: "Triple Jump, Omidyar Network and MetLife Foundation serve as partners and co-owners of the fund." Structure: 0M equity tranche held by Habitat (51% majority) + Triple Jump (manager, partly owned by Oxfam Netherlands) + Omidyar Network (PRI) + MetLife Foundation, leveraged ~10x by 0M of OPIC (now DFC) debt at fund launch in 2012. Independent corroboration on metlife.com/sustainability/MetLife-Foundation/our-work/ which lists MicroBuild as a "Representative Investment" of MetLife Foundation under their Impact Investments portfolio (distinct from grants). Entity disambiguation: Habitat page names "MetLife Foundation" verbatim — the 501(c)(3) charitable arm — not bare "MetLife" or MetLife Investment Management (MIM); MetLife Foundation's own site claims MicroBuild as part of a 2M Impact Investments portfolio distinct from MetLife Inc.'s separate .1B General Account responsible-investment program. Note on Accion Venture Lab Fund II: PR Newswire/Accion newsroom name only bare "MetLife" alongside Mastercard — likely MetLife Inc./MIM, not the Foundation; not relied on. Action taken: microbuild-fund flipped from pending_onboard to active in network/catalogue/impact_funds.csv; LP Page URL set to Habitat program page; new fund_lp_scraper_MicroBuild.py created (3 rows: Triple Jump, Omidyar Network, MetLife Foundation).

Patagonia/Tin Shed Ventures/Holdfast Collective family. Tin Shed (corporate-VC arm) self-describes as having "our sole limited partner, our home planet" — captive Patagonia profits, no outside LPs. Tin Shed portfolio (tinshedventures.com/portfolio/) lists 10 positions: 9 are direct startup equity (Matter, Fibe, Edacious, Nofence, Rubi Labs, Heirloom Carbon, Numat, Trove, Beyond Surface Technologies), 1 is a fund — Mad Capital's Perennial Fund. Verbatim attribution from Mad Capital seed-round PR (prnewswire.com/news-releases/mad-capital-closes-4m-seed-round-..., 14 Dec 2022): "Mad Capital's funds are supported by a global cadre of investors that believe this model can scale worldwide, such as Patagonia's venture capital fund Tin Shed Ventures, Silverstrand Capital, Homecoming Capital, and 38 others." Perennial Fund I closed $10M (2021), Perennial Fund II closed $78.4M (Sep 2024). OUT_OF_SCOPE rationale: (a) US-only farmer-transition credit, falls in US-domestic exclusion; (b) manager Mad Capital PBC is a Boulder-CO domestic B-corp impact lender, not an INGO and not a peer to Oxfam/CARE/Mercy Corps/Accion/Acumen/AgDevCo; (c) thematically adjacent to AgDevCo SDU / Incofin agRIF / IDH Farmfit but at sub-INGO scale and US-only geography. Holdfast Collective check: 501(c)(4) grantmaking model — disclosed disbursements (Marin Community Foundation $20M, TNC $7.2M, Rodale $1.55M, CalTrout $1M) are charity grants, not fund LP commitments. patagonia.com/how-we-fund/ returned Akamai bot-failover, no other LP-disclosure surfaced. Skill-doc presumption "Tin Shed direct-equity / Holdfast grant-only" partially refuted (Tin Shed has 1 fund-LP exposure) but the fund itself is OUT_OF_SCOPE for the INGO/global-South universe. Operator reference: tinshedventures.com/portfolio/ (full Tin Shed list) + patagoniaworks.com/press (Holdfast charity-grant trail).

Vitol Foundation (Geneva charitable, founded 2003; ~$300M total grants since inception, 104 new grants in 2023, ticket sizes £10K–£500K; theme: child health & education in low-income countries) operates as a grant-maker with occasional repayable capital direct to NGOs. Verbatim from vitol-foundation.com: "The Vitol Foundation is a grant-making organisation. It view[s] these as investments and looks for programmes with the potential to generate a social and economic return in a sustainable and scalable way." And: "By supporting local organisations with grants and repayable capital, we aim to strengthen communities and maximise impact." Impact12 investor-database categorises Vitol Foundation as Fund Types = "Grants, Donations (Philanthropy)"; Eligible Business Entities = "Not for profit organizations"; Specific funds/investees = "None named." Searched: vitol.com/about-us/vitol-foundation (HTTP 403 Cloudflare on parent corporate site — but operating foundation site is at vitol-foundation.com), vitol-foundation.com/our-approach + /our-impact, impacteurope.net/members/vitol-foundation, investors.impact12.com/investor/vitol-foundation, vitol-foundation.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/01/Vitol-Foundation-Report-Strategy.pdf (PDF binary, not parsed). Outcomes-fund spot-checks (Education Outcomes Fund, ILFE, Bridges SDG, BIX Capital, BAT SDG, Save the Children Global Ventures, WaterEquity, MicroBuild, Acumen, Goodwell, Heifer) — zero LP attribution. Single ambiguous case: Red Hat Impact "Trade Finance Vehicle for Menstrual Health Enterprises" (Indo-Pacific) names Vitol Foundation as co-supporter alongside DFAT and a Sydney family office, but language is "support" not "limited partner / commitment / anchor"; sub-INGO-impact-fund-universe scale; insufficient evidence for LP attribution. Confirms grant-only posture. Closest cousin of Vitol-style child-health/education philanthropy LPing into outcomes funds is Jacobs Foundation (already MATCH_NEW for ILFE earlier this cycle) — Vitol does not appear there.

IKEA Social Entrepreneurship B.V. (Netherlands-based, founded 2018, Inter IKEA Group's social-business support arm; distinct from IKEA Foundation Stichting and INGKA Foundation). Operating model is exclusively direct support (grants + loans + occasional minority equity) into ~100-200 social enterprises in 35 countries (~EUR 35M total deployed since 2018). Verbatim from Impact Europe corporate-impact-investing case study: "supports these ventures in scaling their impact through the combination of grants, loans and equity" — direct investments, not LP commitments. FY24 Annual Review: "We provided nearly EUR 6 million in grants and new loans, a 20 percent increase compared to FY23" — direct grants/loans only. Closest fund-adjacent reference is the Take-a-Stake investment fund (Yunus Social Business / WASTE / SIDA, India waste sector): verbatim from ikeasocialentrepreneurship.org/en/partnerships/accelerating-for-impact/take-a-stake — "Our grant to the Yunus Social Business will be used to pilot, along with WASTE and SIDA, the setup of the Take-a-Stake investment fund." This is a GRANT to support fund setup, not an LP commitment to a fund. Searched: ikeasocialentrepreneurship.org (newsroom + annual review + partnerships pages), ikea.com/global/en/our-business/sustainability/social-entrepreneurship, impacteurope.net case study, "IKEA Social Entrepreneurship" + LP/limited-partner/fund-investor/anchor-investor cross-checks against tracked impact-fund universe — zero LP attributions. Disambiguation confirmed: all sources naming the entity verbatim use "IKEA Social Entrepreneurship" (Inter IKEA, Delft); IKEA Foundation Stichting is a separate vehicle and would surface under its own foundation_lps.yml entry if not already on /network/. Confirms direct-investment-only posture; recommend operator skip this foundation for /network/ LP rendering. Could potentially render as direct portfolio-company investor if/when portco-investor disclosure for individual social enterprises is in scope.

Checked: Global Fund Innovative Finance page (Debt2Health, blended finance with MDBs, RED, results-based financing) — Global Fund is recipient of capital or outcomes-payer to grantees, not LP. Verified absent from GHIF LP roster (https://ghicfunds.org/global-health-investment-fund/) and Transform Health Fund LP roster (https://fsdafrica.org/press-release/transform-health-fund-raises-50m-to-scale-proven-innovative-healthcare-models-in-africa/) — both candidate health funds. Steering Group Partner of Health Finance Coalition (HFC) but not THF LP. India TB blended finance is a 0m grant against a 00m World Bank loan buy-down (not LP). South Africa IMAGINE SOBC outcome funder is SA DSI, not Global Fund. Triple I for GH is convening, not a fund. Multilateral public charity that pools sovereign-donor + Gates Foundation contributions and disburses grants to country implementers; treasury reserves managed conservatively by World Bank as trustee.

Checked: GPE Multiplier (https://www.globalpartnership.org/funding/gpe-multiplier) — grant-matching mechanism, not LP. GPE Girls Education Accelerator + Debt2Ed are GPE-deployed grants/concessional lending, not LP into external funds. Education Outcomes Fund (EOF) — GPE NOT an outcome funder; EOF is hosted by UNICEF, founded by Education Commission + GSG; named outcome funders are FCDO, KOICA, BofA, Hempel Foundation. EOF described as operating ALONGSIDE GPE (peer multilateral). ILFE — Jacobs Foundation + SDC anchored, no GPE. Educate Girls DIB — CIFF + UBS Optimus, no GPE. Quality Education India DIB — Dell Foundation + British Asian Trust, no GPE. SDG Outcomes Fund — EU/EIB anchor + BII/DFC/UBS Optimus, no GPE. GPE design: World Bank-administered multi-donor trust fund (FIF), channels sovereign donor capital INTO GPE then OUT as grants to country governments via grant agents (World Bank, UNICEF). GPE is recipient of sovereign commitments, not LP into anything. EOF is interesting as a potential standalone tracked fund but GPE is not its LP.

Verbatim: Grand Challenges Canada (with funding from Global Affairs Canada) listed alongside Royal Philips, IFC, Swedfund, US DFC, Proparco, MSD, FSD Africa Investments, ImpactAssets, GHIC, Ceniarth, UBS Optimus Foundation, Skoll Foundation, Chemonics, Anesvad Foundation, Netri Foundation, USAID. GCC catalytic-pool quote: "Our $5M USD investment represented over 25%% of the catalytic capital pool" (https://www.grandchallenges.ca/what-we-do/how-we-fund/investment-funds/). THF: AfricInvest-managed blended-finance debt/mezzanine fund, $111M final close 3 October 2024, focus African healthcare supply chains/care delivery/digital health; co-managed with Health Finance Coalition. Slug transform-health-fund added to peer_funds.yml as a Non-INGO impact fund (comparable); Pipeline Status flipped to active; scraper fund_lp_scraper_TransformHealthFund.py created (17 LPs scraped from GCC final-close page). GCC also LPs into GHIF ($108M, 2012) and incubated CBIV ($90M womens & childrens health, 2024) per same GCC investment-funds page — not added in this batch (GHIF is older 2012-vintage and may have wound down; CBIV is plausible future addition).

IDH operates as fund manager (Farmfit, &Green) and TA-facility manager (AGRI3, LDN), not as an LP into peer funds. Checked: idhsustainabletrade.com (AGRI3 page = TA Manager only); LDN Fund LP roster (EIB/AFD/DEFRA/etc, no IDH); idh.org/investment-solutions (own funds only); IDH 2024 Annual Report (own vehicles only). No fund-of-funds activity disclosed. Recommend modeling IDH as fund-manager / TA-manager node only.

Verbatim: "the Schmidt Family Foundation announced their collective investment into Aligned Climate Capital's Aligned Climate Fund 2 LP (ACF2), bringing the fund's total commitments to approximately $70 million." Rockefeller Foundation press release 2024-08-01. Co-LPs: Nathan Cummings Foundation, Rockefeller Foundation. Slug aligned-climate-fund-2 added to peer_funds.yml under Non-INGO comparables (validation_status: unverified pending final close confirmation); scraper fund_lp_scraper_AlignedClimateFund2.py created. 11thhourproject.org/impact-investing also independently lists Aligned Climate Capital as investee.

Argidius operates as grant-funder to BDS / SGB ecosystem (ANDE, SGB Evidence Fund, GALI, CFF, Mowgli, Aceli), not as a fund LP. Checked: argidius.com / porticus.com Argidius pages (grant framing only); andeglobal.org/writer/argidius-foundation; Aspen Argidius-ANDE Finance Challenge (prize, not LP); theigc.org/initiatives/sgb-evidence-fund (co-funder); Truvalu/Acumen LatAm/Symbiotics SGB/Bamboo BLOC/Adobe Capital cross-checks (no LP disclosure). Textbook BDS / ecosystem-grant funder pattern.

Azim Premji Trust (philanthropic endowment, distinct from Premji Invest family office) is a named LP in Avaana Climate and Sustainability Fund (final close Oct 2024, USD 135M). Verbatim (BusinessToday 2024-10-23): "Other major supporters include the US International Development Finance Corporation (DFC), the UK Government UK-India Development Cooperation Fund (UKIDCF), the Self Reliant India (SRI) Fund, Small Industries Development Bank of India (SIDBI), Azim Premji Trust, and several large corporations." Cross-confirmed by Climate Insider, YourStory, Entrepreneur India, Business Standard. OUT_OF_SCOPE: Avaana is a mainstream domestic Indian climate-tech VC manager with no INGO parent (LPs are DFIs + corporates), not a peer of the INGO impact-fund universe. APF/APPI is overwhelmingly grants-only to Indian education NGOs; Avaana is the only public LP commitment surfaced in 6 fetches. No tracked fund (Aavishkaar India VI, ACF2, Mercy Corps Ventures, Acumen, LeapFrog, Omnivore) names APF/APPI/Trust as LP.

Fundacion Cisneros (Patricia Phelps de Cisneros & Gustavo Cisneros, Caracas/NY) is education + arts patronage only (AME, Piensa en Arte, ACUDE, Cl@se programs; CPPC art collection at MoMA). No LP commitments to any impact fund found in 7 fetches. Disambiguated from for-profit Cisneros entities (Cisneros Group, Cisneros Real Estate, Cisneros Capital, American Triple I) which make direct equity investments not in scope; from Fundacion Tropicalia (CSR arm of Cisneros Real Estate) which partners with IDB/MIF on grant-funded SME inclusion in Miches DR (technical assistance grants, not fund LP); from unrelated Gilbert & Jacki Cisneros Foundation (US-domestic education grants); and from Henry Cisneros (former HUD secretary). Cross-checked LP lists for ALIVE/Acumen LatAm (IDB Lab, DGGF, MacArthur, Bancoldex, Bancolombia Fdn, Sura Fdn, WWB Fdn) and EcoEnterprises IV (IDB Invest, FinDev Canada, Visa Foundation, Wire Group, FMO) - Cisneros not on either. No 990-PF surfaces (Venezuelan/international, not US 501c3).

Helmsley Charitable Trust (~USD 8B AUM) operates a direct-PRI program, not a fund-of-funds LP program. Across ~8 fetches: (1) Reaching the Last Mile Fund (USD 22.5M + 4.13M) is a multi-donor pooled grant facility (END Fund), not an investment fund with LP units. (2) TIXiMED PRI (USD 2.65M Dec 2024 + 2.2M expansion Mar 2026) is a direct loan to a pharma company. (3) ~30 direct equity/loan PRIs visible in CB Insights/Tracxn/SWFI (e.g., Capillary Biomedical exit Jul 2022) - none are fund LP positions. (4) PitchBook LP profile paywalled but aggregator reference is to Sequoia Capital (mainstream VC, not impact). (5) ProPublica 990-PF FY22 schedule shows "All Other Program Related Investments" without fund-level detail. (6) Cross-checked African Agricultural Capital Fund / Pearl Capital / Yield Uganda (LPs: Rockefeller, USAID, Gatsby, Gates, JPMC) - Helmsley not named. (7) Cross-checked Transform Health Fund, WaterEquity, &Green, AgDevCo, Acumen, Heifer - no Helmsley LP role found. Pattern: US foundation with PRI program routed through direct underwriting (TIXiMED, Capillary Biomedical) rather than fund LP exposure. Suggest mri_program: direct_pri_only flag in foundation_lps.yml as distinguishing signal vs. Rockefeller/Gates which DO commit to managed funds.

Heron Foundation (F.B. Heron) publicly LPs into US-domestic community-development and SMB private-equity funds (SJF Ventures, DBL Bay Area Equity Fund, Pacific Community Ventures, Community Reinvestment Fund, Bridges US arm per 2017 990-PF, Greenline Ventures, Southern Appalachian Fund, Penn Ventures Partners) plus CDFIs (Reinvestment Fund, LISC, Self-Help, Boston Community Capital, Calvert Impact Capital). All US-only — outside the INGO/EM impact-fund universe. Checked: heron.org/investments (404), heron.org/news (404), conscious-portfolio-construction-2 (only listed-equities managers Ownership Capital/Aperio/BlackRock named), heron.org/enterprise (refers to intermediaries generically), 2015 Mission-Related Investing PDF (unreadable via fetch), CDFI Fund overview PDF (timed out), InfluenceWatch secondary, NCFP 2017 IPS, plus 4 web searches against Acumen, Root Capital, MicroBuild, WaterEquity, Bridges SDG Outcomes Fund — none surface a primary-source quote naming Heron as LP. Calvert Impact Community Note already tracked as blocked.

Verbatim (KLF co-founder Charly Kleissner page): "KLF also supports smaller and first-time social venture funds that need blended capital for themselves or for supporting capacity building for their investees. Examples are investments in Global Partnerships, Grassroots Business Fund and Acumen Capital Markets I." KLF references the 2006-2017 100% impact portfolio era (no specific commitment year stated). Created new scraper fund_lp_scraper_GBF.py against Charly Kleissner site (verified status 200, needle present). Other named KLF holdings (DBL, Bridges, Generation, Better Ventures, Beartooth, Aqua-Spark, Eko, MicroVest I, RSF, Sonen, ImpactAssets, Total Impact Capital) are mainstream impact-VC / DAF intermediaries outside our universe; partial confirmation of skill-doc OUT_OF_SCOPE flag, but GBF edge is a clean MATCH_TRACKED.

Lankelly Chase Foundation announced July 2023 it is dissolving and redistributing ~£130-138M over 5 years (rejecting "spend down" framing in favor of "movement-aligned capital strategies" / direct redistribution to communities, e.g. £8M to Baobab Foundation) rather than new LP commitments. Only primary-sourced fund-style investment is the 2010 Peterborough Social Impact Bond (one-off bespoke project SIB via GoLab/INDIGO directory entry INDIGO-ORG-0640) — not a tracked fund. Bridges Fund Management 2023 Annual Report (PDF) shows no Lankelly mentions. UBS/Bridges SDG Outcomes Fund LP disclosure names DFC, BII, Legatum, Tsao, Ferd — not Lankelly Chase. Checked 7 fetches: Google search, lankellychase.org.uk/investments (Cloudflare 403), insight/how/investments (403), Pitchbook (403), GoLab INDIGO, Bridges 2023 AR, targeted SDG Outcomes/Bridges Outcomes/Big Issue Invest/Resonance searches. Foundation's own /investments/ page is bot-blocked; flag for revisit if disclosure surface changes.

Lemelson's PRI portfolio is publicly disclosed at lemelson.org/funding/ and consists of invention-focused early-stage VC funds (predominantly India/Kenya hardware/medtech) plus US climate-tech accelerators: Ankur Capital, Menterra, Jaza Rift One, TEAMFund, PRIME Coalition / Azolla Ventures Catalytic Fund I, Unitus Seed Fund, Ventureast, VilCap Investments, Third Derivative, VertueLab. None match any tracked fund or fit the INGO-affiliated / microfinance / blended-finance / outcomes-fund peer profile. Sources checked: lemelson.org homepage, /funding/ (full PRI list extracted), /impact/, plus web search 'Lemelson Foundation impact investing portfolio MRI PRI fund limited partner'.

McConnell's published Mission-Related and Program-Related Investment portfolio at mcconnellfoundation.ca/how-we-invest/our-portfolio/ is dominated by Canadian community-development, affordable housing, Indigenous capital, and Canadian climate-tech funds (Raven Indigenous Impact Fund I & II, New Market Funds Rental Housing Fund I/II, Amplify Capital II LP, Renewal3, Fonds Investissement Montreal IV, plus Crossborder Impact Ventures, Cycle Capital, InvestEco, Greensoil, Eiffel, Fair Finance Fund, Kaleidoscope, Efficiency Capital, etc.). One UK Bridges PE/property holding (Bridges Property Alternatives Fund V / Bridges Sustainable Growth Fund V) is distinct vehicle from `bridges-sdg-outcomes-fund`. None of ~30 disclosed fund holdings overlap with our tracked INGO/global-impact-fund universe; foundation's stated goal is 'to help build a thriving impact investing market in Canada' — Canadian-community-development bucket per rubric.

NoVo Foundation is fundamentally a grant-making private foundation (Hudson Valley-focused after 2020 restructuring) with no public evidence of being an LP in any tracked impact fund. Only fund-vehicle association is anchor donation to Abundance Circle — a fund of funds structured as a donor-advised fund administered by ImpactAssets (philanthropic catalytic capital, not traditional LP commitment); Abundance Circle is not in our tracked universe and no underlying portfolio funds are publicly disclosed. Tracked women's funds (IIX WLB, WWB, CARE-SheTrades, SWIFT, British Asian Trust outcomes) do not list NoVo on investor lists. Checked novofoundation.org, /news/, web searches for 'limited partner / LP / PRI / fund commitment', cross-referenced IIX WLB investor list (Ford, Minderoo, Paul Ramsay, IFC, ABP — no NoVo). ImpactAlpha Abundance Circle article was 403 (Cloudflare). Pattern: grants-only foundation.
